texto: I am not a courageous person by nature. I have simply discovered that, at certain key moments in this life, you must find courage in yourself, in order to move forward and live. It is like a muscle and it must be exercised, first a little, and then more and more. All the really exciting things possible during the course of a lifetime require a little more courage than we currently have. A deep breath and a leap.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a John Patrick Shanley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “I am not a courageous person by nature. I have simply discovered that, at certain key moments in …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que coragem, bravery, vida pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de coragem e vida.

texto: I want to say to you: Doubt can be a bond as powerful and sustaining as certainty.
explicacao: Citação de John Patrick Shanley sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I want to say to you: Doubt can be a bond as powerful and sustaining as certainty.” articula reflexao, honesty, doubt em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: If I could, Sister James, I would certainly choose to live in innocence. But innocence can only be wisdom in a world without evil. Situations arise and we are confronted with wrongdoing and the need to act.
explicacao: Citação de John Patrick Shanley sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “If I could, Sister James, I would certainly choose to live in innocence. But innocence can only b…” articula reflexao, evil, good em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como good e good-vs-evil.
